title: Review: Eval canonicalization coverage (16 suggestions from task 229)
analysis:
Value::Matrix(Vec<Vec<Value>>) was introduced by task 229 and merged into main (commit 41849de7), but a regression in commit 3a248e07 stripped Value::Matrix and all associated methods (canonicalize_matrix, try_into_matrix) from value.rs entirely. Type::Matrix still exists in ty.rs (restored by task 231), but Value::Matrix is completely absent from the current codebase at HEAD 72bad5d6.

The 16 review suggestions from esc-229-16 were triaged in esc-229-15 into 7 DO NOW items and 9 SKIP items. All suggestions reference code that no longer exists due to the regression.

Plan: Restore Value::Matrix and all supporting code via TDD, applying the 7 DO NOW review fixes at implementation time rather than restoring the original buggy code and then patching. The SKIP items are addressed via documentation comments.

DO NOW items addressed: #4 (content_hash comment), #5 (guard clone on Matrix variant in sum), #9 (Matrix*Vector test), #10 (format_value_matrix test), #11 (List.sum() with Matrix test), #12 (empty-matrix round-trip test), #16 (reorder canonicalize before undef).

SKIP items documented: #1 (comment on eval_method_call coverage), #2/#14 (doc on empty matrix limitation), #3 (module boundary trade-off doc note), #6 (single-pass try_into_matrix applied from start), #7 (efficient format_value from start), #8 (try_into_matrix retained with doc note), #13 (fold caveat comment), #15 (jagged matrix deferred).

Regression scope: Value::Matrix variant, canonicalize_matrix(), try_into_matrix(), Matrix arms in Display/PartialEq/Ord/content_hash, Matrix arm in format_value (analysis.rs), Matrix arm in classifier.rs, Matrix canonicalization in eval_binop/eval_unop, Matrix canonicalization in eval_method_call sum path.

prerequisites.description: Add Value::Matrix(Vec<Vec<Value>>) variant to the Value enum in value.rs (after Tensor, before Complex) with doc comment: 'User-facing matrix literal (m rows x n cols). Before arithmetic evaluation, canonicalized to nested Value::Tensor (rank-2) via canonicalize_matrix(). This method exists to support the reify-expr evaluator pipeline.' Also add Matrix to exhaustive match in classifier.rs (in the other-data arm alongside Enum/List/Set/Map/Option/Lambda/Field/Tensor/Complex). Also add Matrix arm to infer_value_type in builders.rs (panic with 'literal() cannot infer Matrix type. Use CompiledExpr::literal(value, type) directly.' like Tensor).

Create crates/reify-expr/tests/tensor_matrix_arithmetic_tests.rs test file scaffold with imports (reify_types::{Value, Type, BinOp, UnOp, CompiledExpr, DimensionVector, ValueCellId}, reify_expr::{eval_expr, EvalContext}, reify_types::persistent::PersistentMap as ValueMap) and a helper function matrix_2x2(a,b,c,d) that builds Value::Matrix(vec![vec![Value::Real(a),Value::Real(b)],vec![Value::Real(c),Value::Real(d)]]).

steps.description: Write failing tests for Value::Matrix construction, PartialEq, and Display in value.rs test module. Tests: (a) matrix_construction_and_partial_eq — two identical 2x2 matrices are equal, different ones aren't; (b) matrix_display — Value::Matrix(vec![vec![Int(1),Int(2)],vec![Int(3),Int(4)]]).to_string() == '[[1, 2], [3, 4]]'; (c) matrix_ne_tensor — Matrix([[1,2]]) != Tensor([Tensor([Int(1),Int(2)])]).

Add Matrix arms to PartialEq and Display impls in value.rs. PartialEq: (Value::Matrix(a), Value::Matrix(b)) => a == b. Display: nested bracket format — write '[', for each row: write '[', join elements with ', ', write ']', join rows with ', ', write ']'.

Write failing tests for Value::Matrix content_hash in value.rs test module. Tests: (a) matrix_content_hash_determinism — same 2x2 matrix hashes identically across two calls; (b) matrix_content_hash_differs_from_nested_tensor — Matrix([[1,2],[3,4]]) hash != Tensor([Tensor([Int(1),Int(2)]),Tensor([Int(3),Int(4)])]) hash (tag 18 vs tag 14).

Add Matrix content_hash arm with tag=18 and two-level encoding (DO NOW #4). Code: tag(18) + combine(row_count) + for each row: combine(col_count) + combine(element hashes). Add documentation comment: '// Hash structure: tag(18) + row_count + for each row: col_count + element hashes. // This two-level encoding intentionally differs from Tensor's flat tag(14)+count+elements // to distinguish Matrix from nested-Tensor at the hash level.'

Write failing tests for Value::Matrix Ord in value.rs test module. Tests: (a) matrix_ord_after_complex — Matrix (type_tag=15) > Complex (type_tag=14); (b) matrix_ord_within_type — compare two matrices with different rows, verify lexicographic order.

Add Matrix to Ord impl in value.rs. In type_tag(): Value::Matrix(_) => 15. Update type_tag comment to: 'Undef=0, Bool=1, Int=2, Real=3, Scalar=4, String=5, Enum=6, List=7, Set=8, Map=9, Option=10, Field=11, Lambda=12, Tensor=13, Complex=14, Matrix=15'. In within-type match: (Value::Matrix(a), Value::Matrix(b)) => a.cmp(b).

Write failing tests for canonicalize_matrix() in value.rs test module. Tests: (a) canonicalize_2x2 — Matrix([[Real(1),Real(2)],[Real(3),Real(4)]]).canonicalize_matrix() == Tensor([Tensor([Real(1),Real(2)]),Tensor([Real(3),Real(4)])]); (b) canonicalize_empty_outer — Matrix(vec![]).canonicalize_matrix() == Tensor(vec![]); (c) canonicalize_identity_real — Real(1.0).canonicalize_matrix() == Real(1.0); (d) canonicalize_identity_tensor — Tensor([Int(1),Int(2)]).canonicalize_matrix() == Tensor([Int(1),Int(2)]).

Implement pub fn canonicalize_matrix(self) -> Self on Value. Match Value::Matrix(rows) => Tensor(rows.into_iter().map(Value::Tensor).collect()), other => other. Add doc comment: '/// Convert a Value::Matrix to nested Value::Tensor (rank-2 Tensor where each element is a Tensor row). All other variants are returned unchanged. /// /// This method exists to support the reify-expr evaluator pipeline. The module boundary /// coupling (reify-types knowing about reify-expr conventions) is an accepted design trade-off.'

Write failing tests for try_into_matrix() in value.rs test module. Tests: (a) round_trip_non_empty — Matrix([[Int(1),Int(2)],[Int(3),Int(4)]]).clone().canonicalize_matrix().try_into_matrix() == Some(original_matrix); (b) empty_outer_round_trip_returns_none (DO NOW #12) — Matrix(vec![]).canonicalize_matrix().try_into_matrix() == None, with comment: 'Documents known invariant break: round-trip fails for empty matrices'; (c) non_tensor_returns_none — Real(1.0).try_into_matrix() == None; (d) flat_tensor_returns_none — Tensor([Real(1.0)]).try_into_matrix() == None.

Implement pub fn try_into_matrix(self) -> Option<Self> on Value. Use single-pass approach: match Tensor(rows) if !rows.is_empty() => rows.into_iter().map(|r| match r { Value::Tensor(elems) => Some(elems), _ => None }).collect::<Option<Vec<_>>>().map(Value::Matrix), else None. Add doc comment: '/// Convert a rank-2 nested Value::Tensor back to Value::Matrix. /// Returns Some(Matrix(...)) if self is a non-empty Tensor where every element is a Tensor. /// Returns None otherwise. Note: the round-trip invariant canonicalize_matrix().try_into_matrix() /// holds only for non-empty matrices. Empty Matrix canonicalizes to Tensor([]) which returns None here. /// Currently used in tests for round-trip verification; retained for future LSP/format use.'

Write failing tests for Matrix canonicalization through eval_binop and eval_unop in tensor_matrix_arithmetic_tests.rs. Tests: (a) matrix_plus_matrix — BinOp::Add on two 2x2 Matrix literals (matrix_2x2(1,2,3,4) + matrix_2x2(10,20,30,40)) produces Tensor([Tensor([Real(11),Real(22)]),Tensor([Real(33),Real(44)])]); (b) scalar_times_matrix — BinOp::Mul on Real(2.0) * matrix_2x2(1,2,3,4) produces scaled nested Tensor; (c) neg_matrix — UnOp::Neg on matrix_2x2(1,2,3,4) produces Tensor with negated elements.

Add Matrix canonicalization in eval_binop and eval_unop in reify-expr/src/lib.rs. In eval_binop: after let lv = eval_expr(left, ctx); let rv = eval_expr(right, ctx); add let lv = lv.canonicalize_matrix(); let rv = rv.canonicalize_matrix(); BEFORE the undef check (consistent with DO NOW #16 ordering pattern). In eval_unop Neg arm: add Value::Matrix(_) => pattern that canonicalizes first (let v = v.canonicalize_matrix(); then fall through to existing Tensor match), or restructure as: first canonicalize v at the top of eval_unop for all ops, then proceed with existing match.

Write Matrix*Vector canonicalization test in tensor_matrix_arithmetic_tests.rs (DO NOW #9). Test: matrix_literal_times_tensor_vector — BinOp::Mul on Value::Matrix(vec![vec![Real(1),Real(2)],vec![Real(3),Real(4)]]) lhs (Type::matrix(2,2,Type::Real)) and Tensor([Real(1),Real(1)]) rhs (Type::tensor(1,2,Type::Real)), assert result equals Tensor([Real(3), Real(7)]) — the dot products [1*1+2*1, 3*1+4*1]. Should pass with step-12 canonicalization in eval_binop.

Write failing test for List.sum() with Matrix elements in tensor_matrix_arithmetic_tests.rs (DO NOW #11). Test: list_sum_matrix_elements — build Value::List(vec![matrix_2x2(1,0,0,1), matrix_2x2(2,3,4,5)]), wrap in MethodCall expr with method='sum' and result_type=Type::tensor(2,2,Type::Real), assert result == Tensor([Tensor([Real(3),Real(3)]),Tensor([Real(4),Real(6)])]).

Add Matrix canonicalization in eval_method_call sum path in reify-expr/src/lib.rs with three review fixes: (1) DO NOW #5 — guard clone+canonicalize on Matrix variant: for initial acc, use let mut acc = if matches!(items[0], Value::Matrix(_)) { items[0].clone().canonicalize_matrix() } else { items[0].clone() }; For loop items, use if matches!(item, Value::Matrix(_)) { let item = item.clone().canonicalize_matrix(); if item.is_undef() { return Value::Undef; } acc = eval_add(&acc, &item); } else { if item.is_undef() { return Value::Undef; } acc = eval_add(&acc, item); }. (2) DO NOW #16 — for Matrix items, canonicalize before undef check (already reflected in pattern above). (3) SKIP #1 — add comment at top of eval_method_call: '// Note: only .sum() dispatches to eval_add on raw values within this function. // Other methods (count, contains, fold, map, filter, etc.) do not perform direct // arithmetic on their elements. If adding new arithmetic methods, add canonicalization. // eval_binop handles canonicalization for all operator-based arithmetic paths.'

Write failing test for format_value with Matrix in analysis.rs test module (DO NOW #10). Test: format_value_matrix — assert format_value(&Value::Matrix(vec![vec![Value::Int(1), Value::Int(2)], vec![Value::Int(3), Value::Int(4)]])) == "[[1, 2], [3, 4]]".

Add Matrix arm in format_value in analysis.rs. Use direct String buffer approach (applying SKIP #7 from the start): Value::Matrix(rows) => { let mut out = String::from("["); for (i, row) in rows.iter().enumerate() { if i > 0 { out.push_str(", "); } out.push('['); for (j, elem) in row.iter().enumerate() { if j > 0 { out.push_str(", "); } out.push_str(&format_value(elem)); } out.push(']'); } out.push(']'); out }. Insert after the Tensor arm.

Add documentation comments for remaining SKIP items: (1) On fold return in eval_method_call (after acc is returned, ~line 573): '// Note: fold may return a raw Value::Matrix if the lambda produces one. // Callers consuming fold results in arithmetic contexts should canonicalize, // or route through eval_binop which handles canonicalization.'; (2) Verify all doc comments from prior steps are present: canonicalize_matrix module boundary note (step-8), try_into_matrix empty limitation and test-only note (step-10), eval_method_call coverage note (step-15).

design_decisions.decision: Restore Value::Matrix with review fixes applied from the start rather than restoring original buggy code then patching
design_decisions.rationale: Value::Matrix was lost in a regression (commit 3a248e07). Rather than replaying the original task-229 code and then applying the 7 DO NOW fixes, we build the corrected version directly via TDD. This avoids introducing known bugs even temporarily and produces cleaner commit history.

design_decisions.decision: Use content_hash tag=18 for Matrix and Ord type_tag=15
design_decisions.rationale: Content hash tag 18 is consistent with the original task-229 implementation. Tags 0-15 are taken by existing variants (Undef through Complex). Ord type_tag 15 follows Complex(14) as next sequential. If other lost variants (Point, Vector, Range, Orientation) are restored by other tasks, tag numbering will need coordination — Matrix at 15 leaves room above for future types.

design_decisions.decision: Guard canonicalize_matrix clone on Matrix variant only in sum() path
design_decisions.rationale: DO NOW #5 identified that unconditional clone+canonicalize for every item in sum() is a performance regression for the common case (lists of Ints/Reals/Tensors). Since canonicalize_matrix is identity for non-Matrix, guarding with matches!(item, Value::Matrix(_)) avoids unnecessary heap allocation. The branch prediction cost is negligible vs allocation cost.

design_decisions.decision: Document empty-matrix round-trip limitation rather than fixing try_into_matrix
design_decisions.rationale: The round-trip canonicalize_matrix().try_into_matrix() returns None for empty matrices (Matrix(vec![]) -> Tensor(vec![]) -> None). Triaged as SKIP (#2/#14): fixing would require accepting ambiguity between empty Tensor and empty Matrix, or adding a smart constructor. An explicit test (DO NOW #12) documents the None result as intentional.

design_decisions.decision: Canonicalize in eval_binop rather than blanket-canonicalizing all method call arguments
design_decisions.rationale: Suggestion #1 (SKIP) proposed blanket canonicalization at eval_method_call entry. Rejected because only sum() does arithmetic on raw values; other methods (count, contains, set ops, map, filter) don't need it. Canonicalizing in eval_binop catches ALL binary arithmetic paths uniformly. A comment documents which method paths are and aren't safe with Matrix inputs.

design_decisions.decision: Canonicalize in eval_binop AFTER evaluating both sides but BEFORE the undef check
design_decisions.rationale: Placing canonicalization after eval but before undef check ensures Matrix values are converted to Tensor before any arithmetic dispatch or undef short-circuit. This matches the ordering pattern from DO NOW #16 (canonicalize before undef check in sum). canonicalize_matrix is a no-op for Undef, so the ordering is functionally equivalent but self-evidently correct.

design_decisions.decision: Apply single-pass try_into_matrix and efficient format_value from the start
design_decisions.rationale: SKIP #6 (single-pass try_into_matrix) and SKIP #7 (String buffer format_value) were marked skip because the existing code wasn't hot-path. However, since we're implementing from scratch, the efficient versions are equally simple to write. No reason to implement less efficient versions that would need future cleanup.

reuse.how: Follow the existing Tensor hash pattern (tag + combine(count) + element hashes) but add an inner row-count per row for the two-level Matrix structure. Reuse ContentHash::of() and combine() methods.
Matrix Display uses nested bracket format. Inner row formatting reuses the same '[', join-with-comma, ']' pattern as Tensor's existing Display arm.

After canonicalize_matrix() converts Matrix to nested Tensor, all existing Tensor arithmetic paths (component-wise add/sub, scalar*tensor mul, tensor/scalar div) handle the computation. No new arithmetic implementations needed.
Tests use CompiledExpr::literal(Value::Matrix(...), Type::matrix(m,n,q)) directly since infer_value_type cannot infer Matrix type. Binop/Unop expressions built with existing builder functions.
Matrix format_value follows List/Tensor pattern (bracket wrapping + recursive format_value calls) but nests two levels.

reuse.what: MethodCall expression building pattern from collection tests
reuse.how: Existing sum tests demonstrate how to build MethodCall expressions with object + method name + args + result_type. Reuse this pattern for the Matrix sum test (step-14).
Use Type::matrix(m, n, quantity) to construct result types for Matrix-valued expressions in tests. Already exists from task-229/task-231.
